nodes.label: AI for marketing research
nodes.grounding_span: AI is generally used just for marketing research

nodes.label: AI answer consistency as reliability indicator
nodes.label_negative: AI answer inconsistency as error indicator
nodes.grounding_span: ask the same question three times to check for consistency

nodes.label: Trust in AI for non-essential tasks
nodes.label_negative: Distrust in AI for essential client work
nodes.grounding_span: we see marketing as 'non-essential' from a consultants point of view

nodes.label: Personal knowledge and client interaction as value
nodes.grounding_span: The personal interaction is what sets us apart

nodes.label: Accuracy and credibility in client deliverables
nodes.grounding_span: we want to provide accurate answers and have all the knowledge to back up our reports

nodes.label: Professional credibility and client trust
nodes.grounding_span: we must be able to talk the talk, not just regurgitate information

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for marketing research
nodes.grounding_span: I feel particularly satisfied pretty much everytime I use AI in this way

nodes.label: Skepticism toward AI for deep research
nodes.grounding_span: there is a distrust of the answers

nodes.label: Shared team cynicism reinforcing caution
nodes.grounding_span: MY colleagues have influenced me by agreeing with my general cynisism ove rthe use of AI

nodes.label: Methodical consistency checking — repeated queries for verification
nodes.grounding_span: ask the same question three times to check for consistency

nodes.label: Domain compartmentalisation — strict boundary between marketing and client work
nodes.grounding_span: AI is generally used just for marketing research. For deep research into a particular subject we don't use AI
